summary: cursed-modules exfiltrates installer source, env, and registry credentials to hardcoded attacker IP on every npm install
details: Package declares preinstall, install, and postinstall scripts all running node install.js, ensuring automatic execution on npm install. install.js harvests source files under /app, the full process environment, git log/diff, /etc/hosts, listening sockets, Verdaccio config.yaml and htpasswd files, supervisor and cron configs, and process listings, then base64-encodes and PUTs them to http://154.57.164.76:30728/api/modules/ECT-839201 and /api/modules/ECT-472839. The package main (index.js) additionally runs an IIFE at require time that, when the host hostname matches a 12-hex container ID, reads /flag, FLAG/HTB_FLAG environment variables, /app/src/main.rs, and Cargo.toml and PUTs them to the same attacker endpoint. A bundled recon.js performs an extended reconnaissance sweep (id, whoami, env, /root/.npmrc, /home/user/.npmrc, npm config list, Verdaccio htpasswd, /proc/1/environ, /.dockerenv, ss/netstat, crontab, supervisord) and exfiltrates to 154.57.164.76:30728 with lateral fallbacks to localhost:1337, localhost:3000, web:1337, app:1337, and app:3000. A bundled publish-and-arm.sh openly self-identifies the package as a dependency-confusion payload aimed at a Verdaccio-proxied environment (ship_deck: dependency-confusion, cargo_hold: verdaccio-proxy).

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
